The Alien Who Built His Own Language: The Yeat Story 🔔👽🚜

Yeat reflects a newer kind of stardom, one built through internet momentum, underground mythology, and a sound that felt instantly alien. Born Noah Olivier Smith on February 26, 2000, in Irvine, California, he spent time in Fullerton before moving to the Portland, Oregon area, where he attended Lakeridge High School. He comes from a mixed background that has become part of his story too: his mother is Romanian, and his father is American

Kurt Cobain passed away 32 years ago today. He would have been 59. Few artists have shifted culture the way he did. 🕊️🧔🏼‍♀️ 

It’s hard to overstate Kurt Cobain’s impact on music and culture. As the frontman of Nirvana, he became the voice of a generation that felt disconnected from what came before it. His writing was raw, personal, and unfiltered, shifting alternative music from the margins into the mainstream.

With the release of Nevermind, Cobain helped redefine the sound of the early 90s.

Objects That Will Outlive Any Algorithim: Why Physical Media Is So Important Today 📀🫁 

Physical media represents a different relationship with culture, rooted in ownership, intention, and permanence. Before everything lived in the cloud, music, films, and publications existed as objects you could hold, collect, lose, and rediscover. Vinyl records, cassette tapes, DVDs, and print magazines carried not just content, but context liner notes, cover art, sequencing, and imperfections that shaped the

If you respect real lyricism and authentic storytelling, this is someone you need to watch.

When children play with letters and form new words, they stretch their thinking, explore different possibilities, and begin to see patterns in language. Activities like this encourage problem-solving, creativity, and flexible thinking as children try different combinations to discover new words.

It’s a playful way to challenge young minds, boost vocabulary, and inspire children to think beyond the obvious.
